project.name: openfire-app
project.description: OpenFire is a Tauri v2 desktop GUI for browsing, querying, and managing Firestore databases, with a React + TypeScript + Vite + Tailwind frontend and a Rust backend that accesses Firestore over gRPC using service accounts. Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

nodes.name: worktrees.json
nodes.summary: Cursor editor worktree configuration declaring a setup-worktree entry for the project.

nodes.name: release.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow that builds and publishes signed Tauri desktop release artifacts on tagged releases.
nodes.languageNotes: Tauri release workflows typically matrix across OS targets to produce platform-specific bundles.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Project guide documenting OpenFire's architecture, key directories, development commands, and mandatory TDD testing conventions.

nodes.name: components.json
nodes.summary: shadcn/ui configuration defining component style, Tailwind integration, and import path aliases.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Vite HTML entry point that mounts the React application root and loads the main TypeScript bundle.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Frontend package manifest defining npm scripts, React/Tauri dependencies, and dev tooling for the OpenFire app.

nodes.name: postcss.config.js
nodes.summary: PostCSS configuration enabling Tailwind CSS and Autoprefixer plugins for the build pipeline.

nodes.name: build.rs
nodes.summary: Cargo build script that invokes tauri_build::build() to generate Tauri context and metadata at compile time.

nodes.name: default.json
nodes.summary: Tauri v2 capability manifest granting the main window its permitted set of plugin and core permissions.

nodes.name: Cargo.toml
nodes.summary: Rust crate manifest for the Tauri backend, declaring the firestore crate and other gRPC/runtime dependencies.

nodes.name: ic_launcher.xml
nodes.summary: Android adaptive launcher icon resource referencing foreground and background drawable layers.

nodes.name: ic_launcher_background.xml
nodes.summary: Android color resource defining the launcher icon background fill.

nodes.name: commands.rs
nodes.summary: Core Tauri command layer exposing all Firestore operations (CRUD, query, import/export, transfer, emulator/connection management) to the frontend via IPC.
nodes.languageNotes: Async Tauri commands wrap blocking Firestore calls via run_blocking/spawn_blocking and convert JSON to/from Firestore document values.

nodes.name: set_active_account
nodes.summary: Activates a stored service account by loading its metadata, opening a FirestoreDb connection, and registering it in app state.

nodes.name: list_collections
nodes.summary: Lists collection IDs under a parent path with pagination support, returning a CollectionList.

nodes.name: list_documents
nodes.summary: Fetches a page of documents in a collection and maps them to FirestoreDocument models.

nodes.name: save_document
nodes.summary: Upserts a document, creating it when absent or updating it when present, then returns the serialized result.

nodes.name: duplicate_document
nodes.summary: Copies a source document to a target path with optional overwrite handling.

nodes.name: duplicate_collection
nodes.summary: Copies every document from a source collection into a target collection, respecting an overwrite flag.

nodes.name: delete_collection
nodes.summary: Deletes all documents within a collection by iterating and removing each by ID.

nodes.name: query_documents
nodes.summary: Runs a structured query against a collection and returns matching documents as models.

nodes.name: build_query_params
nodes.summary: Translates a QuerySpec into FirestoreQueryParams, assembling limits, order-by clauses, and composite filters.

nodes.name: build_filter_compare
nodes.summary: Maps a single QueryFilter operator and value into a Firestore comparison filter.

nodes.name: export_collection
nodes.summary: Recursively exports a collection to a pretty-printed JSON file on disk.

nodes.name: import_collection
nodes.summary: Reads a JSON file and imports its entries into a collection, validating each entry's __id__ field and honoring the import mode.

nodes.name: connect_emulator
nodes.summary: Opens a FirestoreDb connection against a local emulator URL for the given project and registers it in app state.

nodes.name: transfer_documents
nodes.summary: Transfers documents from a collection in one connection to a collection in another, with overwrite handling.

nodes.name: export_collection_recursive
nodes.summary: Walks a collection and its subcollections recursively, building a nested JSON representation.

nodes.name: for_each_doc_in_collection
nodes.summary: Iterates over every document in a collection, applying a handler callback to each.

nodes.name: parse_collection_path
nodes.summary: Parses a slash-delimited collection path into a parent reference and collection ID.

nodes.name: parse_document_path
nodes.summary: Parses a slash-delimited document path into parent, collection ID, and document ID components.

nodes.name: json_to_value
nodes.summary: Recursively converts a serde_json::Value into a typed Firestore value, handling all primitive and nested container types.

nodes.name: credentials.rs
nodes.summary: Manages service account credentials on disk: importing key files, listing accounts, and reading project/email metadata.

nodes.name: CredentialManager
nodes.summary: Encapsulates storage and retrieval of service account credential files and their derived metadata.

nodes.name: save_from_path
nodes.summary: Imports a service account key file from a path, storing it and returning a summary of the account.

nodes.name: list
nodes.summary: Enumerates stored credential files and returns summaries for each known service account.

nodes.name: parse_metadata
nodes.summary: Extracts project ID and client email metadata from a raw service account JSON.

nodes.name: emulator.rs
nodes.summary: Defines an EmulatorTokenSource that supplies a stub auth token for connecting to the local Firestore emulator.

nodes.name: error.rs
nodes.summary: Defines the AppError enum used as the unified error type across the Rust backend.

nodes.name: lib.rs
nodes.summary: Tauri application entry point that wires up state, the credential manager, the crypto provider, and registers all IPC command handlers.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Builds and launches the Tauri application, managing shared state and registering the full invoke handler command set.

nodes.name: main.rs
nodes.summary: Binary entry point that delegates to the library crate's run() function to start the Tauri app.

nodes.name: models.rs
nodes.summary: Defines serializable data models shared across the IPC boundary: documents, collections, query specs, filters, and connection/transfer results.

nodes.name: state.rs
nodes.summary: Holds the application's shared connection state, tracking active and multiple FirestoreDb connections and their modes.

nodes.name: AppState
nodes.summary: Central mutable app state managing the active FirestoreDb handle plus a registry of named connections.

nodes.name: tauri.conf.json
nodes.summary: Tauri v2 application configuration defining window settings, bundle identifiers, and build commands.

nodes.name: emulator_audit.rs
nodes.summary: Integration test that connects to a running Firestore emulator and exercises write/read/list/query round-trips.

nodes.name: emulator_connect_write_read_list_query
nodes.summary: End-to-end emulator test verifying a document can be written, read back, listed, and queried.

nodes.name: App.tsx
nodes.summary: Root React component orchestrating the entire OpenFire UI: layout, sidebar, tabs, dialogs, keyboard shortcuts, and Firestore data wiring.
nodes.languageNotes: Large root component that composes dozens of feature components and Zustand stores into the desktop shell.

nodes.name: App
nodes.summary: Top-level application component wiring stores, hooks, dialogs, and the resizable layout into the running UI.

nodes.name: ShellSplitInner
nodes.summary: Inner layout component arranging the content split between document list and preview panes.

nodes.name: CollectionTree.tsx
nodes.summary: Renders the root collection tree in the sidebar, mapping fetched collection IDs to expandable tree items.

nodes.summary: React component fetching top-level collections and rendering them as CollectionTreeItem nodes.

nodes.name: CollectionTreeItem.tsx
nodes.summary: Expandable collection tree node that lists its documents and exposes a context menu for collection actions.

nodes.name: CollectionTreeItem
nodes.summary: React component rendering an expandable collection node with its documents and a right-click context menu.

nodes.name: DocumentNode.tsx
nodes.summary: Renders a single document node within the collection tree, with expand/collapse state and selection handlers.

nodes.name: DocumentNode
nodes.summary: Document tree row component that manages local expand state and fires selection callbacks for collection/document.

nodes.name: AboutDialog.tsx
nodes.summary: Modal dialog showing app version/about info with actions to copy the repo URL and open it externally via Tauri.

nodes.name: ConnectionManagerDialog
nodes.summary: Renders the connection list and wires switch/remove/import/emulator callbacks for each connection.

nodes.name: CreateDocumentDialog.tsx
nodes.summary: Dialog for creating a new Firestore document with an ID field and JSON payload, with validation before submit.

nodes.name: CreateDocumentDialog
nodes.summary: Manages document-id and JSON-payload form state, validates the JSON, and submits the new document.

nodes.name: DeleteConfirmDialog.tsx
nodes.summary: Confirmation dialog requiring the user to type the target name before deleting a collection or document.

nodes.summary: Type-to-confirm deletion dialog that gates the destructive action on matching the target path's name.

nodes.name: DuplicateCollectionDialog.tsx
nodes.summary: Dialog for duplicating a collection to a target path with an optional overwrite toggle.

nodes.name: DuplicateCollectionDialog
nodes.summary: Captures target-path and overwrite form state, validates input, and submits the collection duplication.

nodes.summary: Dialog for duplicating a document into a target collection with a new id and overwrite option.

nodes.name: DuplicateDocumentDialog
nodes.summary: Manages collection/document-id form state derived from the source doc path and submits the document duplication.

nodes.name: EmulatorConnectDialog.tsx
nodes.summary: Dialog to connect to a local Firestore emulator by entering a project id and emulator URL.

nodes.name: EmulatorConnectDialog
nodes.summary: Captures project-id and emulator-URL inputs and submits an emulator connection request.

nodes.name: ImportCollectionDialog.tsx
nodes.summary: Dialog for importing a collection from a file, with a native file picker and merge/overwrite mode selection.

nodes.name: ImportCollectionDialog
nodes.summary: Handles file selection via a native open dialog and import-mode state, then submits the collection import.

nodes.name: SaveNamedItemDialog.tsx
nodes.summary: Generic dialog prompting for a name to save a labelled item, reused by the save-query and save-script dialogs.

nodes.name: SaveNamedItemDialog
nodes.summary: Reusable name-prompt dialog that validates a trimmed name and invokes the onSave callback.

nodes.name: SaveQueryDialog.tsx
nodes.summary: Thin wrapper around SaveNamedItemDialog preconfigured to save a query by name.

nodes.name: SaveScriptDialog.tsx
nodes.summary: Thin wrapper around SaveNamedItemDialog preconfigured to save a script by name.

nodes.name: ShortcutsHelpDialog.tsx
nodes.summary: Help dialog that groups keyboard shortcuts by category and renders each with a formatted key combination.

nodes.name: ShortcutsHelpDialog
nodes.summary: Groups the SHORTCUTS list by category and renders formatted key bindings in the help dialog.

nodes.name: TransferDialog.tsx
nodes.summary: Dialog for transferring a collection from the source connection to another connection's destination path.

nodes.name: TransferDialog
nodes.summary: Selects a destination connection and collection path and submits a cross-connection collection transfer.

nodes.name: DocumentListSection.tsx
nodes.summary: Document list panel with search, infinite-scroll pagination, table/list view modes, and per-document actions.

nodes.name: DocumentListSection
nodes.summary: Renders the document list with loading/error/empty states, search input, infinite scroll, and create/duplicate/delete actions.

nodes.name: DocumentPreviewSection.tsx
nodes.summary: Document detail/preview panel with JSON and fields editing modes, draft persistence, dirty tracking, and save/duplicate/delete.

nodes.name: DocumentPreviewSection
nodes.summary: Editable document preview managing editor/fields drafts, dirty-state notifications, and save-on-keypress with JSON validation.

nodes.name: fmtTime
nodes.summary: Formats a timestamp string for display by normalizing the date-time separator.

nodes.name: DocumentRow.tsx
nodes.summary: Single document list row with selection, a context dropdown menu, and copy/duplicate/delete actions.

nodes.name: DocumentRow

nodes.summary: Typed field editor that detects a value's Firestore type and renders the appropriate type-specific input control.

nodes.summary: Detects the field type, offers a type-change menu, and resets to a default value when the type changes.

nodes.name: FieldValueEditor
nodes.summary: Renders and parses a type-specific value input (string, number, boolean, array, map, geopoint, etc.) for a field.

nodes.name: CommandPalette.tsx

nodes.summary: Renders the palette groups from store state and runs the selected command, closing the palette afterward.

nodes.name: docId
nodes.summary: Extracts the trailing document id segment from a Firestore document path.

nodes.name: ContentSplit.tsx
nodes.summary: Two-pane resizable content split using the default layout sizing hook.

nodes.name: ContentSplit
nodes.summary: Renders left/right panes within a resizable split panel using default layout sizes.

nodes.name: ErrorBoundary.tsx
nodes.summary: React error boundary that catches render errors, logs them, and shows a reload fallback UI.

nodes.name: ErrorBoundary

nodes.summary: Top-level sidebar/main resizable layout wrapper using the default layout sizing hook.

nodes.summary: App sidebar showing the active account, connection status, and the collection tree with management actions.

nodes.summary: Bottom status bar rendering a list of status items on the left and optional right-aligned content.

nodes.summary: Tab bar driven by the tabs store, rendering open document/collection tabs with activate/close and new-tab controls.

nodes.summary: Renders tabs from the tabs store with activate/close handlers and a new-tab button.

nodes.name: Toolbar.tsx
nodes.summary: Top toolbar for the collection view rendering breadcrumb navigation, view-mode toggle, and query/shell panel toggles with collection actions.

nodes.name: OnboardingState.tsx
nodes.summary: Empty-state onboarding screen prompting the user to import a service account, connect to the emulator, or restore a backup when no connection exists.

nodes.name: OnboardingState
nodes.summary: Renders the first-run call-to-action cards wiring import, emulator-connect, and restore-backup callbacks.

nodes.name: FilterRow.tsx
nodes.summary: Single where-clause editor row for the query builder, providing field, operator, and value inputs plus the supported Firestore operator list.

nodes.summary: Single order-by clause editor row letting the user pick a field and toggle ascending/descending sort direction.

nodes.name: OrderByRow
nodes.summary: Controlled row editing an orderBy clause's field and direction, emitting onChange/onRemove callbacks.

nodes.name: QueryBar.tsx
nodes.summary: Compact query summary bar showing active filters as badges with run/clear/save actions and an expandable QueryBuilder.

nodes.name: QueryBar
nodes.summary: Displays the active query as removable filter badges and expands into the full QueryBuilder for editing.

nodes.name: QueryBuilder.tsx
nodes.summary: Full Firestore query builder managing filter and order-by rows plus a limit, parsing raw input values and assembling a query to run.

nodes.name: parseFilterValue
nodes.summary: Coerces a raw filter input string into a number, JSON array, or string for use as a Firestore filter value.

nodes.name: QueryBuilder
nodes.summary: Stateful builder that adds/updates/removes filter and order-by rows, syncs from an initial query, and assembles the final query on run.

nodes.name: SavedQueryList.tsx
nodes.summary: Renders the list of saved queries with load and delete actions for each entry.

nodes.name: SavedQueryList
nodes.summary: Maps saved queries to clickable rows that invoke onLoad and onDelete callbacks.

nodes.name: ScriptPanel.tsx
nodes.summary: Interactive scripting panel that runs user scripts against a Firestore DB API via the script engine and renders timestamped output.

nodes.name: ScriptPanel
nodes.summary: Wires the script store and view store to a code editor, executes scripts through createDbApi/executeScript, and appends output entries.

nodes.name: badge.tsx
nodes.summary: shadcn/ui Badge primitive with variant styling and an optional status dot.

nodes.summary: shadcn/ui ContextMenu primitives wrapping Radix context menu for right-click actions.

nodes.summary: shadcn/ui Dialog primitives wrapping Radix dialog with header/footer layout helpers.

nodes.summary: Reusable empty-state presentation component rendering an icon, title, description, and optional action with tone variants.

nodes.summary: Small Kbd primitive rendering keyboard shortcut hints with consistent styling.

nodes.summary: shadcn/ui Popover primitives wrapping Radix popover (trigger, anchor, content).

nodes.summary: shadcn/ui ScrollArea primitives wrapping Radix scroll area with a custom scrollbar.

nodes.summary: shadcn/ui Select primitives wrapping Radix select with trigger, content, item, and scroll buttons.

nodes.name: separator.tsx
nodes.summary: shadcn/ui Separator primitive wrapping Radix separator for visual dividers.

nodes.name: sheet.tsx
nodes.summary: shadcn/ui Sheet primitives wrapping Radix dialog as a slide-in side panel with header/footer helpers.

nodes.summary: shadcn/ui Table primitives (header, body, row, cell, caption) for styled HTML tables.

nodes.summary: shadcn/ui Tabs primitives wrapping Radix tabs (list, trigger, content).

nodes.name: toast.tsx
nodes.summary: shadcn/ui toast primitive built on Radix Toast, exporting the provider, viewport, title, description, close, action components and variant styles.

nodes.name: toaster.tsx
nodes.summary: Renders the active toast queue from the useToast hook, mapping each toast to a styled Toast with a variant-specific icon.

nodes.name: use-toast.ts
nodes.summary: Headless toast state manager with a reducer, dispatch, removal queue, and useToast hook driving the global toast notification system.

nodes.name: CellEditor.tsx
nodes.summary: Inline cell editing component for the table view, dispatching to per-type editors (scalar, boolean, timestamp, reference, geopoint) with keyboard commit/cancel/advance handling.

nodes.name: FieldsView.tsx
nodes.summary: Recursive field tree editor for a Firestore document, rendering expandable rows with add/delete and nested change propagation.

nodes.name: TableView.tsx
nodes.summary: Spreadsheet-style table view of Firestore documents with column detection, inline cell editing, pending-edit tracking, infinite scroll, and keyboard navigation.

nodes.name: ViewModeToggle.tsx
nodes.summary: Small tab toggles for switching the document list display mode and preview mode, backed by the view store.

nodes.name: firestore.ts
nodes.summary: TanStack Query hooks wrapping the Tauri Firestore IPC commands for listing collections, paginated documents, single documents, queries, and delete mutations.

nodes.name: use-keyboard-shortcuts.ts
nodes.summary: React hook that binds global keydown handlers, matching events to declared shortcuts while ignoring editable targets unless always-allowed.

nodes.name: cell-flush.ts
nodes.summary: Flushes pending inline cell edits from the cell-edits store into Firestore documents, applying values at paths and collecting per-document failures.

nodes.name: dialog-utils.ts
nodes.summary: Wrapper around the Tauri dialog plugin to open a native file picker and normalize its single/multi-selection result.

nodes.name: error-utils.ts
nodes.summary: Normalizes unknown thrown values into a human-readable error message string with a fallback.

nodes.name: field-types.ts
nodes.summary: Firestore field-type utilities: detects a value's type, supplies default values per type, and immutably sets/deletes values at nested paths.

nodes.name: firestore-utils.ts
nodes.summary: Path helpers that normalize Firestore paths and derive a parent collection path from a document path.

nodes.name: glass-constants.ts
nodes.summary: Shared glassmorphism style constants and helpers (backdrop, borders, shines, shadows, accent shadow) used across dialogs and surfaces.

nodes.name: keyboard-shortcuts.ts
nodes.summary: Declares the app's keyboard shortcut registry plus helpers to match a keyboard event against a shortcut and format shortcuts for display.

nodes.name: script-completions.ts
nodes.summary: Provides Monaco editor autocomplete items for the db scripting API and registers a completion provider for the script console.

nodes.name: script-engine.ts
nodes.summary: Builds a sandboxed db API over Tauri IPC and executes user scripts with captured console logging for the scripting console.

nodes.name: tauri.ts
nodes.summary: Typed IPC wrapper layer exposing every Tauri backend command (accounts, collections, documents, queries, import/export, emulator, connections) as async functions.
nodes.languageNotes: Thin one-line invoke wrappers giving the frontend a typed surface over Rust commands.

nodes.name: toast-utils.ts
nodes.summary: Convenience helper that shows a destructive error toast with a normalized error message.

nodes.name: utils.ts
nodes.summary: The shadcn `cn` class-name merge helper combining clsx and tailwind-merge.

nodes.name: main.tsx
nodes.summary: React application entry point that mounts the App component into the DOM and imports global styles.

nodes.name: auth-store.ts
nodes.summary: Zustand store managing service accounts and emulator connections: loading, importing, selecting, connecting/disconnecting, and validating the active account.

nodes.name: cell-edits-store.ts
nodes.summary: Zustand store tracking pending inline cell edits keyed by document, with set/clear operations consumed by the table view and flush logic.

nodes.name: connection-store.ts
nodes.summary: Zustand store managing the list of saved Firestore connections including loading, removing, and switching the active connection.

nodes.name: reducer
nodes.summary: Pure reducer handling ADD, UPDATE, DISMISS, and REMOVE toast actions, enforcing the toast limit and queuing removals.

nodes.name: toast
nodes.summary: Creates a toast with a generated id and returns handles to update or dismiss it.

nodes.name: useToast
nodes.summary: React hook subscribing components to the global toast state and exposing the toast/dismiss API.

nodes.name: ScalarEditor
nodes.summary: Inline editor for string and number cells with validation and keyboard commit/cancel/advance handling.

nodes.name: BoolEditor
nodes.summary: Inline true/false picker editor with arrow-key selection and outside-click commit.

nodes.name: TimestampEditor
nodes.summary: Inline editor combining a calendar date picker and a time input to commit a Firestore timestamp.

nodes.name: ReferenceEditor
nodes.summary: Inline editor for Firestore document references with collection-path autocomplete suggestions.

nodes.name: GeopointEditor
nodes.summary: Inline editor for geopoint cells capturing latitude and longitude with validation and outside-click commit.

nodes.name: FieldsView
nodes.summary: Top-level document field tree that wires up add/change/delete handlers and renders a FieldRow per key.

nodes.name: FieldRow
nodes.summary: Recursive expandable row rendering a single field and its nested children with type detection.

nodes.name: formatCellValue
nodes.summary: Formats an arbitrary Firestore field value into a display string, handling arrays, timestamps, and scalars.

nodes.name: detectColumns
nodes.summary: Scans up to a bounded number of documents to derive a sorted set of column names.

nodes.name: TableView
nodes.summary: Main table component rendering documents in a grid with inline editing, pending edits, flash feedback, infinite scroll, and keyboard navigation.

nodes.name: ListModeToggle
nodes.summary: Tab toggle that reads and updates the list display mode in the view store.

nodes.name: PreviewModeToggle
nodes.summary: Tab toggle that reads and updates the preview mode in the view store.

nodes.name: useConnectionKey
nodes.summary: Derives a stable React Query cache key from the active account and connection state.

nodes.name: useDocumentsInfinite
nodes.summary: Infinite query hook paginating documents in a collection via the listDocuments IPC command.

nodes.name: useDeleteDocument
nodes.summary: Mutation hook deleting a document and invalidating affected collection and document queries.

nodes.name: useDeleteCollection
nodes.summary: Mutation hook deleting an entire collection and invalidating related caches.

nodes.name: useKeyboardShortcuts
nodes.summary: Hook that registers a document keydown listener dispatching to handlers when a registered shortcut matches.

nodes.name: flushTablePending
nodes.summary: Applies all pending cell edits to their documents, saves them, clears the store, and aggregates failures.

nodes.name: openFileDialog
nodes.summary: Opens a native file dialog via the Tauri plugin and returns the normalized selected path(s).

nodes.name: getErrorMessage
nodes.summary: Coerces an unknown error value into a readable message, falling back to a default.

nodes.name: detectFieldType
nodes.summary: Classifies a JS value into a Firestore field type (string, number, bool, array, map, timestamp, etc.).

nodes.name: defaultValueForType
nodes.summary: Returns a sensible default value for a given Firestore field type.

nodes.name: setValueAtPath
nodes.summary: Immutably sets a value at a nested object/array path, recursing through path segments.

nodes.name: deleteValueAtPath
nodes.summary: Immutably removes a value at a nested object/array path, recursing through path segments.

nodes.name: normalizeFirestorePath
nodes.summary: Trims, filters, and rejoins slash-separated Firestore path segments into a canonical path.

nodes.name: hexToRgb
nodes.summary: Parses a hex color string into an r,g,b channel string.

nodes.name: matchesShortcut
nodes.summary: Tests whether a keyboard event matches a shortcut definition, honoring platform modifier conventions.

nodes.name: formatShortcut
nodes.summary: Formats a shortcut definition into a human-readable label using platform-specific modifier symbols.

nodes.name: getDbCompletionItems
nodes.summary: Returns the static list of db scripting API completion items for the Monaco editor.

nodes.name: registerDbCompletions
nodes.summary: Registers a Monaco completion item provider that serves the db API suggestions.

nodes.name: createCollectionRef
nodes.summary: Builds a chainable collection reference (where/doc/get) over Tauri IPC for the scripting API.

nodes.name: executeScript
nodes.summary: Executes a user-provided script string against the db API, capturing console output via the onLog callback.

nodes.name: dialog-store.ts
nodes.summary: Zustand store managing global dialog/modal open state across the app.

nodes.name: doc-drafts-store.ts
nodes.summary: Zustand store holding in-progress document draft edits before they are committed to Firestore.

nodes.name: nav-store.ts
nodes.summary: Zustand store tracking the current navigation context (selected project, collection, and document path).

nodes.name: palette-store.ts
nodes.summary: Zustand store controlling the command palette open/close state.

nodes.name: query-store.ts
nodes.summary: Zustand store persisting saved Firestore queries, with helpers to save, delete, and filter queries by collection.

nodes.name: recents-store.ts
nodes.summary: Zustand store tracking recently visited collections/documents for quick navigation.

nodes.name: script-store.ts
nodes.summary: Zustand store persisting user-saved scripts, with save and delete operations.

nodes.name: tabs-store.ts
nodes.summary: Zustand store managing open editor tabs, including per-tab navigation snapshots, activation, and tab opening logic synced with the nav store.
nodes.languageNotes: Snapshots and rehydrates nav-store state per tab to preserve navigation context across tab switches.

nodes.name: view-store.ts
nodes.summary: Zustand store managing the active view mode (e.g. table vs JSON) and related display preferences.

nodes.name: style.css
nodes.summary: Global stylesheet defining Tailwind layers, CSS custom properties for theming, and base/component styles.

nodes.name: test-setup.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest global test setup that installs an in-memory localStorage shim and other browser API mocks for the test environment.

nodes.name: installMemoryStorage
nodes.summary: Installs an in-memory Storage implementation backed by a Map onto a global property to mock localStorage/sessionStorage in tests.

nodes.name: firestore.ts
nodes.summary: TypeScript type definitions modeling Firestore entities such as documents, collections, field values, and query structures shared across the frontend.

nodes.name: tailwind.config.js
nodes.summary: Tailwind CSS configuration defining content paths, theme extensions (colors, fonts, radii), and plugin setup for the shadcn/ui design system.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler configuration enabling strict mode, bundler module resolution, and the @/ path alias for the frontend source tree.

nodes.name: vite.config.ts
nodes.summary: Vite build configuration wiring the React plugin and the @/ path alias for the Tauri frontend dev server and bundle.

nodes.name: vitest.config.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest test runner configuration setting the jsdom environment, global test setup file, and path aliases.

nodes.name: toastError
nodes.summary: No summary available

layers.name: UI Layer
layers.description: React component tree — feature panels (collections, documents, query builder, dialogs, table/field editors, layout, scripting shell) and shadcn/ui primitives, plus the App root and HTML/React bootstrap.

layers.name: State Layer
layers.description: Zustand stores holding client-side state: auth/connection sessions, navigation, tabs, drafts, cell edits, saved queries/scripts, recents, and view/dialog/palette UI state.

layers.name: Data Access Layer
layers.description: React Query hooks and lib modules that bridge the frontend to the Rust backend over Tauri IPC: the typed command wrapper, script engine, Firestore path/field helpers, and cell-flush persistence.

layers.name: Types Layer
layers.description: Shared TypeScript type definitions modeling Firestore documents, collections, field values, and query structures consumed across stores, hooks, and components.

layers.name: Utility Layer
layers.description: Cross-cutting frontend helpers: the cn class-merge utility, error/toast normalization, glassmorphism style constants, and the keyboard-shortcut registry.

layers.name: Rust Backend Layer
layers.description: Tauri Rust backend exposing Firestore operations over IPC — command handlers, credential management, emulator token source, shared data models, connection state, error types, and the build script.

layers.name: Configuration Layer
layers.description: Project and build configuration: package/Cargo manifests, TypeScript/Vite/Vitest/Tailwind/PostCSS configs, Tauri app config, capabilities and icon resources, editor settings, and global styles.

layers.name: Test Layer
layers.description: Test scaffolding and integration tests: the Rust emulator round-trip audit and the Vitest browser-API setup shim.

layers.name: Infrastructure Layer
layers.description: CI/CD pipeline — the GitHub Actions workflow that builds and publishes signed Tauri desktop release artifacts.

layers.name: Documentation Layer
layers.description: Project documentation describing OpenFire architecture, directory conventions, development commands, and the mandatory TDD workflow.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with CLAUDE.md, the project's authoritative guide (there is no README). It explains that OpenFire is a Tauri v2 desktop GUI for browsing, querying, and managing Firestore databases, with a React 18 + TypeScript frontend and a Rust backend that uses the firestore crate over gRPC. Critically, it establishes the security model — all Firebase operations happen in the Rust backend, never in the webview — and the mandatory TDD workflow. Keep this architectural split in mind; the rest of the tour traces how the frontend reaches the backend.

tour.title: Frontend Bootstrap
tour.description: index.html is the Vite entry point that mounts the React root, and src/main.tsx is the JavaScript entry that renders the App component into the DOM and pulls in global Tailwind styles. Together they are the smallest possible shell: load the page, attach React, hand control to App. This is the 'how does it start?' counterpart to the project overview from Step 1.

tour.title: The App Root
tour.description: App.tsx is the orchestrator of the entire UI — it wires together the layout, sidebar, tabs, dialogs, keyboard shortcuts, and Firestore data hooks. With the highest fan-out in the frontend, it imports nearly every store, hook, and feature panel, so reading it gives you a map of the whole application before you dive into any single piece. Treat the imports here as a table of contents for the steps that follow.

tour.title: Shared Firestore Types
tour.description: src/types/firestore.ts is the most depended-upon module in the codebase (highest fan-in). It models documents, collections, field values, and query structures, forming the shared vocabulary that stores, hooks, and components all speak. Understanding these types now makes every later step easier, because the same shapes flow from the Rust backend up through the IPC layer into the UI.
tour.languageLesson: TypeScript interfaces and discriminated unions let a single FieldValue type represent Firestore's many value kinds (string, timestamp, reference, geopoint, map) while keeping each variant type-safe at compile time.

tour.title: The IPC Bridge
tour.description: src/lib/tauri.ts is the single typed boundary between the React frontend and the Rust backend. Every backend operation — accounts, collections, documents, queries, import/export, emulator, connections — is exposed here as an async function wrapping a Tauri `invoke` call. This is the chokepoint the CLAUDE.md security model relies on: nothing touches Firestore directly from the webview, it all funnels through these wrappers.

tour.title: Rust Backend Entry & Commands
tour.description: On the other side of the bridge, src-tauri/src/main.rs delegates to lib.rs, which wires up shared state, the credential manager, and registers every IPC command handler. commands.rs is the heart of the backend, implementing all Firestore CRUD, query building, import/export, transfer, and emulator/connection logic that the tauri.ts wrappers from Step 5 call into. This is where the gRPC firestore-crate work actually happens.
tour.languageLesson: Tauri commands are ordinary Rust functions annotated with #[tauri::command] and registered in an invoke_handler; the framework deserializes JSON arguments from the webview and serializes the Result back, giving end-to-end type safety across the IPC boundary.

tour.title: Backend Support: Credentials, State, Models & Errors
tour.description: These modules support the command layer from Step 6. credentials.rs manages service-account key files on disk and reads project/email metadata; state.rs holds the shared, multi-connection FirestoreDb state; models.rs defines the serializable structs that cross the IPC boundary (mirroring the TS types from Step 4); error.rs unifies all backend failures into one AppError type; and emulator.rs supplies a stub token source for connecting to a local Firestore emulator.

tour.title: Data Hooks & Helpers
tour.description: src/hooks/firestore.ts wraps the tauri.ts IPC wrappers in TanStack Query hooks, adding caching, pagination, and mutation handling for listing collections, fetching documents, running queries, and deleting. It leans on firestore-utils.ts for path normalization and field-types.ts for detecting and defaulting Firestore value types. This is the layer most UI components actually consume, rather than calling IPC directly.
tour.languageLesson: TanStack Query keys queries by a serializable array; co-locating the query key with the IPC call lets the cache automatically dedupe requests and invalidate after mutations like document save or delete.

tour.title: Client State Stores
tour.description: Client-side state lives in small, focused Zustand stores. auth-store and connection-store track service accounts and the active Firestore connection; nav-store and tabs-store drive navigation and open editor tabs; doc-drafts and cell-edits stores hold in-progress edits before they are flushed to Firestore. Separating server state (Step 8's React Query) from client state (these stores) is a deliberate design choice that keeps each concern testable.

tour.title: Layout & Navigation Shell
tour.description: These layout components assemble the window chrome that App.tsx renders. ResizableLayout and Sidebar frame the screen; Sidebar hosts the CollectionTree, which maps fetched collection IDs (via the Step 8 hooks) into expandable tree items. TabBar reflects the tabs-store from Step 9, and CommandPalette provides quick keyboard-driven navigation across collections, actions, and recents. This is the structural skeleton the feature panels plug into.

tour.title: Document Browsing & Editing
tour.description: This is the core feature: viewing and editing Firestore documents. DocumentListSection paginates and searches a collection's documents, switching between list and table modes; TableView offers a spreadsheet-style grid with inline CellEditor edits tracked in the cell-edits store from Step 9. DocumentPreviewSection shows a single document with JSON and field-tree editing, dirty tracking, and save/duplicate/delete, while FieldsView renders the recursive, typed field editor built on the field-types helpers from Step 8.

tour.title: Querying & Scripting
tour.description: Beyond browsing, OpenFire lets power users query and script. QueryBuilder assembles Firestore where-clauses and order-by rows into a query spec (matching the types from Step 4) that QueryBar runs and summarizes, ultimately calling the backend query_documents command. ScriptPanel goes further: script-engine.ts builds a sandboxed `db` API over the same Tauri IPC and executes user scripts with captured console output, turning the GUI into a lightweight Firestore shell.

tour.title: Onboarding & Connection Dialogs
tour.description: When no connection exists, OnboardingState guides the user to import a service account, connect to the emulator, or restore a backup. ConnectionManagerDialog and EmulatorConnectDialog manage saved connections and emulator setup, while TransferDialog moves a collection from one connection to another. These tie the auth/connection stores from Step 9 to the credential and emulator backend modules from Step 7, completing the end-to-end connection story.

tour.title: Configuration & UI Foundations
tour.description: Configuration glues the two halves together: package.json defines the frontend scripts and dependencies, Cargo.toml declares the firestore crate and Rust dependencies, and tauri.conf.json plus capabilities/default.json configure the desktop window and the exact permissions the webview is granted. On the UI side, the shadcn/ui button primitive and the cn class-merge utility are among the most-reused building blocks (high fan-in) underpinning every component you saw above.

tour.title: Testing & Release Pipeline
tour.description: Finally, the capstone: how the project verifies and ships. CLAUDE.md mandates TDD, and emulator_audit.rs is an integration test that connects to a live Firestore emulator to exercise write/read/list/query round-trips, while test-setup.ts installs browser-API shims for the Vitest frontend suite. The GitHub Actions workflow in release.yml builds and publishes signed Tauri desktop artifacts on tagged releases, packaging everything from the previous steps into a distributable app.
tour.languageLesson: GitHub Actions workflows trigger on events (here, release tags), run jobs in parallel, and execute sequential steps; Tauri release builds typically use a matrix across OS targets to produce platform-specific signed installers.
